articles.title: IPL 2026: Rizvi Orange Cap, Bishnoi Purple Cap Race
articles.what_this_means: Yashasvi Rizvi leads the Orange Cap race in IPL 2026 after 12 games, while Yuzvendra Bishnoi holds the Purple Cap as the leading wicket-taker. Both players are in brilliant form with consistent performances that could impact ICC Champions Trophy 2025 selections. Here's your guide to watching India cricket from the US and staying updated on live scores.

articles.title: Bumrah vs Sooryavanshi: RR Chase Top Spot in IPL 2025
articles.what_this_means: Rajasthan Royals are eyeing the IPL 2025 table top as they clash with Mumbai Indians in a high-stakes encounter featuring the battle between Sooryavanshi and Bumrah. With Hardik Pandya returning to MI, this match will decide playoff momentum. US-based cricket fans can catch live coverage on Willow TV and streaming platforms.

articles.title: Aaron Green IPL 2025: Why KKR Must Stick With Star Batter
articles.what_this_means: Aaron Green, IPL's most expensive overseas player, is struggling early in the 2025 season, but Aaron Finch warns KKR not to lose faith by dropping him in the order. Patience, not panic, is the smart play for teams and their overseas stars.

articles.title: EB-2 India May 2026 Visa Bulletin Prediction: Expect Modest Forward Movement
articles.what_this_means: EB-2 India is expected to advance 12-18 days in May 2026, moving the Final Action Date from July 15 to approximately July 27-August 2, 2014. Retrogression risk remains minimal due to timing within the fiscal year, though applicants should monitor for July-September pressure.

articles.title: Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi Delayed to June 2026 Release
articles.what_this_means: Salman Khan's war drama Maatrubhumi is now releasing in June 2026 instead of May after being reimagined from a factual retelling to a fictionalized narrative. The film will hit US theaters first, then stream on platforms like Disney+ Hotstar, Prime Video, or Netflix within weeks. It's shaping up to be one of the biggest new Bollywood releases of summer 2026.

articles.title: OpenAI Alums Launch $100M AI Fund: What It Means for Your Career [2026]
articles.what_this_means: OpenAI alums just launched a $100M fund (Zero Shot) backing the next generation of AI tools. For Indian tech professionals in the US, this means early signals about which AI platforms will dominate, which companies will hire aggressively, and which skills will command salary premiums in 2026.

articles.title: How to Scale AI Projects from Pilot to Production in 2025 [2026]
articles.what_this_means: Most enterprise AI projects fail not because of bad tools, but because companies let pilots sprawl without discipline or production timelines. MassMutual and Mass General Brigham achieved 30% productivity gains and cut help desk resolution from 11 to 1 minute by standardizing tools, defining metrics, and deploying to production—not extending pilots forever. For Indian software engineers, this signals demand for engineers who can move AI from chaos to production, which directly impacts compensation and career growth in 2025.
